title: Sentinel Shield security summary
description: Normalized findings contract consumed by scripts/enforce-gates.sh. Scanner workflows populate this document; the enforcer maps SENTINEL_SHIELD_FAIL_ON_* flags onto summary keys.

summary.description: The enforceable findings. All keys are required so a missing key is an error, never a silent zero.

architecture_violations.description: Architecture-boundary violations, summed across ALL architecture producers (Deptrac, PHPArkitect, dependency-cruiser, ESLint boundaries, custom architecture tests). Detects dependency-boundary breaches — it does not prove domain-modelling quality.

third_party_suspicious_code.description: v0.1.5+ third-party/supply-chain suspicious-code findings (separate channel from app SAST). Optional for backward compatibility.

third_party_install_script_risk.description: v0.1.5+ dependency install-script (pre/post/install) risk findings. Optional.

third_party_obfuscation.description: v0.1.5+ third-party obfuscation indicators (decode→exec, packed blobs). Optional.

third_party_network_behavior.description: v0.1.5+ third-party env-read / outbound-network indicators. Optional.

style_violations.description: v0.1.12+ code-style violations (Pint / PHP-CS-Fixer / PHPCS). Optional.

php_syntax_errors.description: v0.1.12+ PHP lint (php -l) syntax errors. Optional.

dependency_policy_violations.description: v0.1.12+ dependency-policy breaches (license/version/allowlist). Reserved; no default emitter — OSV/Grype/Dependency-Check map to *_vulnerabilities. Optional.

iac_violations.description: v0.1.12+ IaC misconfigurations (Checkov / Conftest / Terrascan). Optional.

dast_findings.description: v0.1.12+ DAST findings (ZAP / Nuclei). Manual/controlled. Optional.

container_image_violations.description: v0.1.12+ built-image checks (Dockle), distinct from Dockerfile lint (unsafe_docker). Optional.

repository_health_warnings.description: v0.1.12+ OpenSSF Scorecard low-scoring checks. Optional.

ai_review_findings.description: v0.1.12+ AI-assisted review findings (Claude Code Security Review / Kuzushi). Non-gating by default. Optional.

required_tool_failures.description: v1.10+ tool-policy counter (additive). Count of required-tool/control failures: required tools that are unavailable / not-configured / execution-error / disabled, plus unsatisfied one-of groups. Emitted only when build-security-summary.sh ran with --profile. NEVER folded into vulnerability counters.

tool_configuration_failures.description: v1.10+ tool-policy counter (additive). Subset: required tools whose status is not-configured or disabled.

tool_execution_failures.description: v1.10+ tool-policy counter (additive). Subset: required tools whose status is execution-error (executable present but no/invalid report).

coverage_threshold_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of coverage thresholds breached (line/branch/method/class), summed across applicable stacks. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0. Separate channel from security counters.

coverage_regression.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. 1 when coverage decreased vs the recorded baseline for ANY applicable stack (0 otherwise). Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

mutation_score_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of mutation-score minimums breached. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

complexity_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of complexity threshold violations. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

duplication_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of duplication threshold violations. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

dead_code_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of dead-code findings that breach policy. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

coverage_line_percent.description: v2.1+ informational. Line coverage percent (aggregate = minimum across applicable stacks — the weakest stack drives the gate). Optional.

coverage_branch_percent.description: v2.1+ informational. Branch coverage percent (aggregate = minimum across applicable stacks). Optional.

coverage_method_percent.description: v2.1+ informational. Method/function coverage percent (aggregate = minimum across applicable stacks). Optional.

coverage_class_percent.description: v2.1+ informational. Class coverage percent (aggregate = minimum across applicable stacks). Optional.

mutation_score_percent.description: v2.1+ informational. Mutation score percent (aggregate = minimum across applicable stacks). Optional.

complexity_max.description: v2.1+ informational. Maximum complexity observed (aggregate = maximum across applicable stacks). Optional.

complexity_average.description: v2.1+ informational. Average complexity (aggregate = maximum across applicable stacks). Optional.

duplication_percent.description: v2.1+ informational. Duplicated-code percent (aggregate = maximum across applicable stacks). Optional.

dead_code_count.description: v2.1+ informational. Count of dead-code items (summed across applicable stacks). Optional.

missing_coverage_evidence.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate (boolean). True when an APPLICABLE coverage tool produced no valid report — so strict/regulated fail on ABSENT coverage, not only on bad coverage. Emitted only when build-security-summary.sh ran with --profile; absent (older/non-profile summary) reads as false. Optional/additive.

changed_lines_coverage_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of stacks whose changed-lines (diff) coverage is below quality.coverage.changed_lines_min. Summed across stacks. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

changed_lines_coverage_percent.description: v2.1+ informational. Changed-lines (diff) coverage percent (aggregate = minimum across applicable stacks). Optional.

missing_test_evidence.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate (boolean). True when an APPLICABLE test stack produced no valid test report. Emitted only with --profile; absent reads as false. Optional/additive.

empty_test_suite.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate (boolean). True when an applicable test report exists but reports zero tests. Emitted only with --profile; absent reads as false. Optional/additive.

test_count.description: v2.1+ informational. Total tests executed (summed across applicable stacks). Optional.

skipped_tests.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate + informational. Count of skipped tests (summed across stacks). Gated in regulated. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

focused_test_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of focused test markers (describe.only/it.only/->only()). Gated in all modes. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

skipped_test_marker_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of skip markers (markTestSkipped/markTestIncomplete/it.skip/xit). Gated in strict/regulated. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

debug_code_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of debug residue (dd/dump/var_dump/console.log/debugger/…). Gated in baseline+. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

large_file_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of files exceeding quality.maintainability.max_file_lines. Gated in strict/regulated. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

large_function_violations.description: v2.1+ engineering-quality gate. Count of functions exceeding quality.maintainability.max_function_lines (best-effort). Gated in strict/regulated. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

missing_architecture_evidence.description: v2.1.0+ architecture-governance gate (boolean). True when an APPLICABLE architecture producer is expected by the profile/policy but produced no valid evidence — status unavailable / not-configured / execution-error / disabled, or no report at all. Emitted only when build-security-summary.sh ran with --profile; absent (older/non-profile summary) reads as false. Optional/additive.

architecture_rule_count.description: v2.1.0+ informational. Number of architecture rules evaluated, summed across producers that expose it (0 when none do). Never a gate.

architecture_tool_count.description: v2.1.0+ informational. Number of architecture producers that produced VALID evidence (status pass or findings), summed across producers. Never a gate.

architecture_context_count.description: v2.1.0+ informational. Bounded contexts / modules / layers detected or declared. Aggregated as the MAXIMUM across producers (they describe the same codebase, so summing would double-count). Never a gate.

production_change_without_test_change.description: v2.2.0+ testing-discipline gate (TDD PROXY). Number of production-change groups where source/application behavior changed without any matching test/spec/feature/acceptance change. A proxy for test-first discipline, NOT proof of it — TDD is a workflow, and a final code snapshot does not record the order its lines were written. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0. Separate channel from security counters.

missing_test_change_evidence.description: v2.2.0+ testing-discipline gate (boolean). True when Sentinel Shield could not compute changed-file evidence for a PR/diff where the profile expects it — no git work tree, no resolvable diff base, or an unreadable/errored report. "We could not compute it" must never read as "no violation". Optional/additive; absent reads as false.

behavior_spec_count.description: v2.2.0+ informational. Number of BDD behavior specs/scenarios detected or executed (spec_count + scenario_count, summed across producers). Counting scenarios says nothing about whether they describe the right behavior. Never a gate. Optional.

missing_behavior_specification.description: v2.2.0+ testing-discipline gate (boolean). True when behavior-specification evidence is EXPECTED (testing_discipline.bdd.enabled + require_behavior_specs, or a required BDD producer in the profile) but absent/unavailable/errored, or a producer ran and declared zero specs and zero scenarios. Never expected by default — a library is not forced to carry BDD. Optional/additive; absent reads as false.

orphan_behavior_specifications.description: v2.2.0+ testing-discipline gate. Number of behavior specs/features with no matching implementation/test evidence, WHEN the producer can determine it (producers that cannot report 0, which is a truthful "cannot determine", not a clean result). Gated in regulated only. Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

acceptance_test_count.description: v2.2.0+ informational. Number of acceptance-level tests/scenarios executed, summed across producers (Playwright, Cypress, Behat/Cucumber at acceptance level). Never a gate. Optional.

acceptance_test_failures.description: v2.2.0+ testing-discipline gate. Number of failing acceptance tests/scenarios, summed across producers. Fails whenever acceptance evidence EXISTS and tests fail (from baseline up). Optional/additive; absent reads as 0.

missing_acceptance_evidence.description: v2.2.0+ testing-discipline gate (boolean). True when acceptance evidence is EXPECTED but absent/unavailable/errored, or a report exists with tests=0 (a suite that ran nothing proves nothing). Passing acceptance tests are not product-owner acceptance. Never expected by default. Optional/additive; absent reads as false.

max_file_lines.description: v2.1+ informational. Largest file line-count observed (aggregate = maximum across stacks). Optional.

max_function_lines.description: v2.1+ informational. Largest function line-count observed (aggregate = maximum across stacks). Optional.

tools.description: Per-tool detail. Tools are optional (not every project has every stack); when present, common fields are validated.

status.description: Tool execution/result state. Legacy: pass/fail/warn/skipped/unavailable. v1.10+ tool-policy states: findings (ran, found issues), not-configured (no config file), not-applicable (stack absent), execution-error (crashed/invalid output), disabled (turned off by policy). Mapping per docs/profile-tool-policy.md: an unavailable/execution-error tool is NEVER rewritten as a clean 0.

tool.description: v1.10+ tool-policy overlay. Effective-profile tool key this entry covers (build-security-summary.sh --profile).

policy.description: v1.10+ effective-profile policy for this tool.

applicability.description: v1.10+ whether the tool's stack applies to the target.

installed.description: v1.10+ a declared executable resolved (best-effort), or the report proves it ran.

configured.description: v1.10+ false when a required config file (e.g. never-touch) is absent.

executed.description: v1.10+ a present+valid report was produced.

gate_enforced.description: v1.10+ true when this tool's status feeds the gate (required + applicable). one-of members / recommended / optional are emitted with false.

report.description: v1.10+ expected raw report path for this tool.

message.description: v1.10+ short human explanation of the derived status.

one_of_groups.description: v1.10+ tool-policy overlay (additive). Echo of the effective-profile one-of groups (e.g. 'tests'): per-group satisfaction so the enforcer can fail an unsatisfied required group. Emitted only with --profile.

targets.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). Target-applicability + coverage for the security acceptance gate (scripts/enforce-security-policy.sh). expected = applicable scan targets; scanned = targets a required scanner actually parsed. A drop is a regression signal.

scanners.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). Per-scanner EXECUTION record consumed by scripts/enforce-security-policy.sh: proves the scanner ran, its version, database freshness (age_days), target applicability, targets scanned, and the raw report digest. Produced by scripts/normalize-security-summary.sh. Optional for backward compatibility.

findings.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). Normalized, per-finding records consumed by scripts/enforce-security-policy.sh for severity/fix/waiver evaluation. Produced by scripts/normalize-security-summary.sh. Optional for backward compatibility.
